intro: Hornemannia viscosa, also known as the sticky hornemannia, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its sticky stems and leaves that can tolerate a wide range of water conditions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
